summary: Shorten `SYMBOL_ID` further to reduce agent token/IO churn: switch from `sym:v2:<sha256_96>` to `sym:v3:<sha256_64>` while keeping the canonical symbol key unchanged.
decisions: Update `SYMBOL_ID` format to `sym:v3:<HEX_SHA256_64>` (16 lowercase hex chars) and update strict validation accordingly.
Keep the canonical symbol key inputs unchanged; only the truncated digest length and version prefix change.
No backwards compatibility: all generated artifacts and docs move to `sym:v3` and must be regenerated together.
pitfalls: This is a breaking ID change; every kb-enabled target repo must regenerate `kb/gen/symbols.jsonl` (and any derived references like `top_symbols`).
Collision risk increases vs 96-bit IDs, but 64-bit truncation of SHA-256 remains extremely unlikely to collide at typical repo symbol counts.
